title: Discovering Brighton: Living & Moving Guide | Wolves
meta: Moving to Brighton? Our local guide covers the city's neighbourhoods, lifestyle and cost of living, plus tips for a smooth Sussex removal.
h1: Discovering Brighton: A Local Guide to Living in the City by the Sea
lead: There is nowhere quite like Brighton. Bohemian and buzzing, with a pebble beach, a famous pier, the unmistakable domes of the Royal Pavilion and a creative, open-minded spirit that draws people from across the country, the city by the sea has long been one of the South East's most sought-after places to call home. If you are thinking of moving to Brighton, you are in good company, and as a Sussex removals team we have helped countless people settle into the city and the towns around it. This local guide explores what makes Brighton special, the character of its different neighbourhoods, what living there really costs, and how to plan a smooth move into one of England's most loved cities. We have been helping people move across Sussex since 2016.
body:
Why Brighton wins people over

Brighton has a personality all of its own. It manages to be a seaside town, a cultural capital, a university city and a commuter base all at once, and somehow holds all of that together with an easy, unpretentious charm. People move here for the sea air and stay for the atmosphere. Before you decide it is the place for you, it helps to understand what gives the city its particular pull.

Life by the sea

The beach is at the heart of everything in Brighton. Whether you are swimming on a summer morning, walking the seafront on a blustery afternoon or simply watching the sun set over the old pier, the sea shapes the rhythm of the city. The promenade, the beachfront cafes and bars, and the open water just steps from the city centre give Brighton a quality of life that landlocked places struggle to match. For many people, that daily contact with the coast is reason enough to move.

A creative, open city

Brighton has long been a magnet for artists, musicians, writers and free thinkers, and that creative energy is woven through the place. The independent shops of the North Laine, the galleries and studios, the festivals that fill the calendar, and the famously welcoming, inclusive spirit of the city make it somewhere people feel they can be themselves. It is one of the most diverse and tolerant cities in the country, and that openness is a large part of its appeal.

Connected yet coastal

For all its seaside character, Brighton is remarkably well connected. Fast trains reach London in around an hour, which has made the city popular with commuters who want sea air at the weekend and the capital within easy reach during the week. Gatwick Airport is a short hop away, and the wider Sussex countryside and South Downs National Park sit right on the doorstep. You get the best of the coast, the city and the country without having to choose between them.

Getting to know Brighton's neighbourhoods

Brighton, together with neighbouring Hove, is a city of distinct areas, each with its own flavour. Where you choose to live makes an enormous difference to your experience, so it is worth spending time getting to know the options before you commit.

The Lanes and North Laine

At the city's heart, the historic Lanes and the bohemian North Laine are all narrow streets, independent shops, cafes and nightlife. Living here puts you in the thick of Brighton's energy, within walking distance of the beach and everything the centre offers. It suits people who want to live at the city's pace and do not mind paying a premium for the location.

Hove for a calmer pace

Just along the coast, Hove offers a more relaxed, slightly more refined version of seaside city life. Wide Regency streets, leafy squares, good schools and a gentler atmosphere make it popular with families and anyone who wants the coast without the constant buzz of central Brighton. Locals will happily tell you it is Hove, actually.

Family-friendly suburbs

Further out, areas such as Preston Park, Fiveways and Withdean offer larger family homes, green space and a quieter, residential feel while keeping the city within easy reach. These neighbourhoods tend to attract families putting down roots, drawn by schools, parks and a sense of community alongside everything the city centre offers a short journey away.

The cost of living in Brighton

It would be misleading to pretend Brighton is cheap. As one of the most desirable places to live on the South Coast, property prices and rents are among the higher in the region, reflecting the demand to live by the sea within commuting distance of London. That said, the city offers genuine value in lifestyle: the beach, the culture and the social scene cost nothing to enjoy, and many residents feel the quality of life justifies the price of living here. As with any move, it pays to budget honestly, weighing housing costs, council tax, transport and the higher prices of a popular city against the income and lifestyle you are gaining.

If Brighton's prices stretch the budget further than you would like, it is worth remembering that the surrounding Sussex towns offer more affordable living within easy reach of the city. We help people move throughout the area, including nearby Worthing, Shoreham and Lewes, all of which put Brighton within comfortable reach while keeping costs more manageable.

Planning your move to Brighton

Moving into a busy, popular city brings its own practical challenges, and Brighton is no exception. Narrow streets, permit parking, tall townhouses with steep staircases and the sheer demand for the area all mean a little planning goes a long way. Get the preparation right and your move into the city by the sea can be as relaxed as a stroll along the prom.

Mind the parking and access

Many of Brighton's most characterful streets are also its tightest, with controlled parking and limited room for a removals van. It is well worth checking parking arrangements at both ends before moving day, and arranging a permit or suspended bay where one is needed. Our experienced crews are used to navigating the city's narrow streets and tall, multi-storey townhouses, and we can advise on access when we quote so there are no surprises on the day. You can see how we handle a full move on our house removals service page.

Book early for the popular dates

Brighton is a sought-after place to move to, and the most popular moving dates, especially around the end of the month and over the summer, get booked up quickly. Securing your removals slot well in advance gives you the widest choice and a calmer run-up. Our guide to planning for a house move sets out a sensible timeline, and you can get a clear, tailored figure through our online quote request, with our removals pricing page explaining how the costs are worked out.

Packing for a city move

Good packing is the difference between a smooth move and a stressful one, and it matters even more when you are carrying boxes up the steep staircases of a Brighton townhouse. Strong, well-filled boxes that stack safely make loading and unloading quicker and protect your belongings along the way. We stock everything you need in our range of moving boxes and packing materials, and if packing a whole home around a busy life feels like too much, our full packing service will take care of the lot.

For anything delicate or valuable, from a framed seafront print to inherited china, our specialist fragile packing service makes sure it arrives in your new Brighton home in perfect condition. Label every box with its contents and the room it belongs in, and unpacking in your new place becomes a pleasure rather than a puzzle, even after a day spent ferrying boxes up and down a tall house.

Storage for a Brighton move

City living often means less space than you are used to, and Brighton's characterful older homes are not always blessed with storage. Whether you are downsizing into a seafront flat, waiting for a completion date to fall into place, or simply need somewhere to keep belongings while you settle in, flexible storage takes the pressure off. Our storage solutions cover both short overlaps and longer arrangements, and our storage size calculator helps you work out exactly how much space you need so you are not paying for room you will not use.

Settling into Brighton life

Once the van has gone and the boxes are in, give yourself time to settle before trying to feel like a local overnight. Set up the essentials, get a good night's sleep, and then start exploring. The quickest way to fall for Brighton is to get out and use it.

Make the city yours

Find your stretch of beach, your favourite cafe in the North Laine, the route you will walk to the station, the pub you will become a regular at. Brighton is a city made for wandering, and every neighbourhood rewards a proper explore. Join a club, get to know your neighbours, and take advantage of the festivals and events that fill the city's calendar. Within a few weeks, the place that felt new on moving day starts to feel like home.

Sort the practicalities

A move into the city comes with the usual list of jobs. Work through these in the first week or two.

  • Register with a local GP and dentist
  • Update your address with your bank, employer, the council and the DVLA
  • Sort out resident parking permits if your street requires them
  • Set up utility accounts and arrange broadband as early as you can
  • Review your home contents insurance for the new address

Moving across Brighton and Sussex

Wherever in the area you are heading, we cover the whole region. We help people move into Brighton and Hove from across the county, and right through East Sussex and West Sussex as well as Surrey, Hampshire and Kent. Whether you are arriving from a nearby town or relocating to Brighton from further afield, including a long-distance move, we can plan it around you. We even handle nationwide and international removals for those leaving the city for pastures new.

The best of Brighton through the seasons

One of the joys of living in Brighton is how the city changes through the year. Summer brings the beach to life, with the seafront thronged, the festivals in full swing and long evenings spent by the water. The Brighton Festival and the Fringe fill May with art, theatre and music across the city, while the famous Pride celebrations turn the streets into a riot of colour. Autumn and winter have their own charm, with bracing walks along an emptier seafront, cosy independent pubs and the city wearing a quieter, more local face once the crowds have gone. Knowing that the city offers something in every season helps newcomers settle in whatever time of year they arrive, and there is always a reason to get out and explore.

Getting around the city

Brighton is a compact, walkable city, and many residents happily live without a car, relying on their own two feet, an excellent bus network and the bicycle. Parking in the centre is limited and often expensive, with controlled zones across much of the city, which is worth bearing in mind both for daily life and for moving day itself. The train station sits a short walk from the centre and connects you to London, Gatwick and the wider Sussex coast. For anyone used to car-dependent living elsewhere, the freedom of a city you can largely cover on foot is one of Brighton's quiet pleasures, and it shapes the relaxed, pedestrian rhythm of daily life by the sea.

Is Brighton right for you?

Brighton is not for everyone, and being honest about that helps you make the right choice. If you crave peace, quiet and plenty of parking, the busier central neighbourhoods may not suit, though Hove and the outer suburbs offer a gentler pace. If you love culture, the sea, independent shops and an open, lively atmosphere, and you do not mind paying a premium for the privilege, few places in the country match it. Many people find the city strikes a near-perfect balance: coastal living, a creative spirit, a strong sense of community and the capital within easy reach. The best way to know is to spend time there before you commit, walking the neighbourhoods and imagining your daily life in each.

Why choose Wolves Removals for your Brighton move

We are a family-run firm based near Pulborough in West Sussex, and we have been helping people move into Brighton and across the county since 2016. We know the city's streets, its narrow lanes and its tall townhouses, and we plan every move to suit the realities of moving into a busy, much-loved coastal city. As a LAPADA member and a Checkatrade-verified company, we treat your belongings with genuine care, and we are fully insured for your complete peace of mind.

Whether you need a full house removal into the heart of the city, careful packing of your most precious things, or flexible storage while you settle in, we will tailor our service to suit your move to Brighton. For more practical advice on moving and local life, browse our collection of moving tips and guides, and when you are ready, give us a call on 01903 893731 or request a quote online. We would love to help you discover Brighton for yourself.
faqs: Is Brighton an expensive place to live?

Brighton is one of the more expensive places on the South Coast, with property prices and rents reflecting strong demand to live by the sea within commuting distance of London. Many residents feel the lifestyle justifies the cost. If the budget is tight, nearby Sussex towns such as Worthing, Shoreham and Lewes offer more affordable living within easy reach of the city.
Can your removals van get into Brighton's narrow streets?

Yes. Our crews are well used to Brighton's tight lanes, controlled parking and tall townhouses with steep staircases. We recommend checking parking at both ends in advance and arranging a permit or suspended bay where needed. We are happy to advise on access when we provide your quote so the day runs smoothly with no surprises.
How far in advance should I book a move to Brighton?

As early as you can. Brighton is a popular place to move to, and the busiest dates, particularly month-ends and the summer, get booked up quickly. Securing your slot well ahead gives you the widest choice of dates and a calmer run-up. Get in touch as soon as your dates are confirmed and we will hold a slot for you.
What is the difference between living in Brighton and Hove?

Central Brighton offers the buzz: the Lanes, the North Laine, nightlife and the beach on your doorstep. Hove, just along the coast, is more relaxed and refined, with wide Regency streets, leafy squares and good schools, which makes it popular with families. Both share the same seafront and excellent connections; it comes down to the pace of life you prefer.
Do you offer storage for a move into Brighton?

We do. City homes often have limited storage, and completion dates do not always line up, so we offer flexible storage to bridge any gap or give you breathing room while you settle in. Your belongings stay clean, dry and secure, and you only pay for the space and time you need. Our online storage calculator helps you estimate the right size.
